target_researchers.name: Mohammad Lotfollahi
target_researchers.collaboration_strategy: Reproduce and improve his methods. Contribute to his open-source tools. Engage via scverse ecosystem.
contact_readiness_criteria: Reproduced at least 2 of his papers with good results
Published or preprinted at least 1 paper in perturbation prediction
Strong GitHub portfolio with 4+ bio-ML repos

target_researchers.name: Fabian Theis
target_researchers.lab: Helmholtz Munich - Institute of Computational Biology
target_researchers.collaboration_strategy: Theis lab is the hub of scverse. Contributing to scanpy/scvi-tools makes you visible to this ecosystem.
contact_readiness_criteria: Understanding of variational inference at a deep level

target_researchers.name: Roser Vento-Tormo
target_researchers.collaboration_strategy: Spatial transcriptomics + GNNs is a natural intersection. Her lab needs ML engineers.

target_researchers.name: Dana Pe'er
target_researchers.collaboration_strategy: Strong ML background. Her lab appreciates rigorous engineering and benchmarking.
contact_readiness_criteria: Trajectory inference or cancer genomics project

target_researchers.name: Marinka Zitnik
target_researchers.collaboration_strategy: Perfect GNN + biology intersection. Her lab actively recruits ML engineers. Contribute to her open-source repos.

target_researchers.name: Cole Trapnell
target_researchers.collaboration_strategy: Monocle ecosystem is important. Biology-heavy but values strong computational contributions.

primary_targets.description: PhD Students & Postdocs in Top Labs — most accessible, code-focused, often overloaded, willing to collaborate
primary_targets.why: Top labs run largely through postdocs and PhD students. These are your actual entry points.
how_to_find: Check lab websites for team members
Look at recent paper author lists (non-PI authors)
Find them on Twitter/X — they are often active
Attend their poster sessions at workshops

secondary_targets.description: Open-Source Maintainers — scverse, scanpy, scVI-tools, PyTorch Geometric, bio foundation model repos
secondary_targets.why: Contributors often become collaborators. Your engineering skills are directly valuable.
how_to_approach: Start with documentation PRs and bug fixes
Engage in issue discussions with substantive suggestions

tertiary_targets.description: Young Faculty Building New Labs — newer assistant professors, newly funded AI-biology labs
tertiary_targets.why: They actively need motivated engineers, infrastructure builders, benchmark contributors.
where_to_find: Recent faculty hires at top institutions
PIs who just got their first big grant

ecosystems.name: scverse
ecosystems.description: One of the MOST important ecosystems in computational biology. Includes scanpy, AnnData, scVI-tools.

ecosystems.name: PyTorch Geometric
ecosystems.description: Extremely relevant for GNN direction. Strong community, well-maintained.

ecosystems.name: Hugging Face
ecosystems.description: Biological foundation models increasingly use this ecosystem. Model hub for scGPT, Geneformer, etc.

key_insight: The fastest way into elite research ecosystems is NOT solo genius papers. It is: strong engineering, reproducibility, reliability, open-source contributions, becoming known in a niche. Especially in computational biology — this field is highly collaborative and infrastructure-driven.
